title: Emtc-u-uci reporting procedures and enhancements
abstract: Certain aspects of the present disclosure relate to methods and apparatus for receiving ACK/NACK reporting in unlicensed IoT systems with long transmission durations. In one example, an eNodeB executes an LBT protocol to gain access to a channel of a shared spectrum, receives radio resource management (RRM) measurement reports from an IoT-U, estimates a number of repetitions used to schedule the IoT-U based on the RRM measurement, and configures the maximum number of HARQ processes that can be scheduled in a frame based on the RRM measurements and the estimate of the repetitions.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed: 1. A method of receiving ACK NACK reporting in unlicensed IoT systems with long transmission durations, comprising: a base station (BS) executing an LBT protocol to gain access to a channel of a shared spectrum; and the BS configuring a maximum number of HARQ processes that can be scheduled in a frame based on the RRM measurements and the estimate of the repetitions. 2. The method according to claim 1, further comprising: the BS matching a number of the HARQ processes to schedule in a frame with the RRM measurement reported by the UE. 3. The method of ACK/NACK reporting according to claim 1 , further comprising bundling a plurality of the HARQ processes. 4. The method according to claim 1, wherein a maximum number of the HARQ processes that can be scheduled in each frame is indicated by a radio resource control (RRC) message. 5. The method of ACK/NACK reporting according to claim 1 , further comprising indicating a maximum number of the HARQ processes in at least one of downlink control information (DCI) or a MAC control element (CE). 6. The method according to claim 1, wherein the frame is self-contained, wherein all the scheduled HARQ processes are ACKed in the same frame. 7. The method of ACK/NACK reporting according to claim 1, wherein the HARQ processes are reported every n frames with the maximum number of the HARQ processes that can be scheduled being reported in said every n frames, wherein n > 1. 8. The method of ACK/NACK reporting according to claim 1, wherein the HARQ processes are reported with a delay, wherein said ACK/NACKs received are for the HARQ processes in a previous said frame. 9. The method of ACK NACK reporting according to claim 1 , wherein the ACK/NACK transmissions are reported on a fallback resource. 10. The method of ACK/NACK reporting according to claim 1 , further comprising: a BS requesting at least one of the ACK/NACK transmissions from said UE using a DCI based trigger. 11. The method of ACK/NACK reporting according to claim 1 , wherein the maximum number of HARQ processes is a function of TDD DL-UL subframe configuration. 12. The method of ACK/NACK reporting according to claim 1 , further comprising! the BS receiving radio resource management (RRM) measurement reports from a user equipment (UE); and the BS estimating of the number of repetitions used to schedule the UE based on the RRM measurement. 13. The method of ACK/NACK reporting according to claim I, further comprising! the BS independently receiving uplink control information (UCI) and at least one physical uplink shared channel (PUSCH), wherein collisions are avoided between said UCI and said at least one TUSCII. 14. The method of ACK/NACK reporting according to claim 1 , comprising: the BS receiving metrics reported by the UE; the BS allocating DL resources to the UE using a PDCCH; the BS sending at least one downlink grant on the PDCCH, wherein the at least one DL grant is a multiple Î¤Î DL grant, and more than one said HARQ process can be jointly scheduled on said multiple TTIs for the UE; the BS sending data to the UE using a PDSCH; and 

 the BS receiving ACK/NACKs for multiple said HARQ processes, one for each said Î¤Î. 15. The method according to claim 14, further comprising: scaling up a transport block size for the at least one of said multiple Î¤Î  grants. 16. A base station to receive ACK/NACK reporting in unlicensed IoT systems with long transmission durations, comprising: a memory; a radio frequency (RF) resource; and a processor coupled to the memory and the RF resource, configured to: execute an LBT protocol to gain access to a channel of a shared spectrum; and configure a maximum number of HARQ processes that can be scheduled in a frame based on the RRM measurements and the estimate of the repetitions. 17. The base station according to claim 16, wherein the processor is further configured to: match a number of the HARQ processes to schedule in the frame with the RRM measurement reported by the UE. 18. The base station according to claim 16, wherein the processor is further configured to: bundle a plurality of said HARQ processes. 19. The base station according to claim 16, wherein the maximum number of HARQ processes that can be scheduled in each said frame is indicated by a radio resource control (RRC) message. 20. The base station according to claim 16, wherein the processor is further configured to: indicate the maximum number of HARQ processes in at least one of downlink control information (DCI) or a MAC control element (CE). 21. The base station according to claim 16, wherein the scheduled HARQ processes are ACKed in the same frame. 22. The base station according to claim 16, wherein the HARQ processes are reported every n frames with the maximum number of the HARQ processes that can be scheduled being reported in said every n frames, wherein n > 1. 23. A method of sending ACK/NACK reporting in unlicensed IoT systems with long transmission durations, comprising: a UE sending radio resource management (RRM) measurement reports to a base station; and the UE receiving a maximum number of the HARQ processes in at least one of downlink control information (DO) or a MAC control element (CE), wherein the maximum number of HARQ processes is a function of TDD DL-UL subframe configuration. 24. The method of ACK/NACK reporting according to claim 23, further comprising: the UE bundling a plurality of the HARQ processes. 25. The method of ACK NACK reporting according to claim 23, wherein the HARQ processes are reported every n frames with the maximum number of the HARQ processes that can be scheduled being reported in said every n frames, wherein n > 1. 26. The method of ACK/NACK reporting according to claim 23, wherein the HARQ processes are reported with a delay, wherein said ACK/NACKs received are for the HARQ processes in a previous said frame. 27. The method of ACK/NACK reporting according to claim 23, wherein the ACK/NACK transmissions are reported on a fallback resource. 28. The method of ACK/NACK reporting according to claim 23, further comprising: 

 the UE independently sending uplink control information (UCI) and at least one physical uplink shared channel (PUSCH), wherein collisions are avoided between said UCI and said at least one PUSCH. 29. The method of ACK NACK reporting according to claim 23, comprising: the UE receiving at least one downlink grant on the PDCCH, wherein the at least one DL grant is a multiple TTI DL grant, and more than one said HARQ process can be jointly scheduled on said multiple TTIs; the UE receiving data on a PDSCH from the BS; and the UE sending ACK NACKs for multiple said HARQ processes to the BS, one for each said TTI. 30. A universal Equipment (UE) to send ACK/NACK reporting in unlicensed IoT systems with long transmission durations, comprising: a memory; a radio frequency (RF) resource; and a processor coupled to the memory and the RF resource, configured to: send radio resource management (RRM) measurement reports to a base station (BS); and receive a maximum number of the HARQ processes in at least one of downlink control information (DCI) or a MAC control element (CE), wherein the maximum number of HARQ processes is a function of TDD DL-UL subframe configuration.
